LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC NTC OF MTN AND MTN FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, ADVANCED FROM SEP-04-2014 TO LAW AND MOTION AT AUG-18-2014 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. PER EX PARTE ORDER FILED ON 7/29. (D302)
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-18-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. - MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION WHICH IS UNOPPOSED. DENIED AS TO THE SEVENTH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PROVIDE EMPLOYEE WAGE AND PERSONNEL RECORDS UPON WRITTEN REQUEST. DENIED AS TO THE 4TH, 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, 6TH AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE GROUNDS FOR A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS MUST APPEAR ON THE FACE OF THE CHALLENGED PLEADING OR FROM ANY MATTER JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE. THE COMPLAINT AND JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE MATTERS, INCLUDING THE ORDER DENYING CLASS CERTIFICATION IN THE BURDA CASE, DO NOT ESTABLISH THAT THIS CASE AND THE BURDA CLASS ACTION ARE NOT BASED ON THE SAME CLAIMS AND SUBJECT MATTER AND SIMILAR EVIDENCE. RATHER, THERE ARE FACTUAL ISSUES THAT MUST BE DETERMINED AS TO WHETHER THE 2 CASES ARE BASED ON SIMILAR EVIDENCE AND WHETHER THERE WAS A RESONABLY FINITE AND LOCATABLE CLASS IN THE BURDA CASE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Aug-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ERIC A . GROVER OF THE KELLER/GROVER LLP, TEL. NO. 543-1305 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CURTIS A. GRAHAM OF THE LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., TEL. NO. (213) 443-4215 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. - Motion is granted as to the 5th cause of action which is unopposed. Denied as to the seventh and 8th causes of action for failure to provide employee wage and personnel records upon written request. Denied as to the 4th, 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 6th and 9th causes of action. The grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings must appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter judicially noticeable. The complaint and judicially noticeable matters, including the Order denying class certification in the Burda case, do not establish that this case and the Burda class action are not based on the same claims and subject matter and similar evidence. Rather, there are factual issues that must be determined as to whether the 2 cases are based on similar evidence and whether there was a resonably finite and locatable class in the Burda case. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)
